inspectorTitle: HAAG Certified Inspector — Residential Roofs (HAAG Engineering)
purposeNote: This report is a contractor scope submission documenting the physical condition of the property following the storm event referenced above. It presents observed damage, applicable building-code requirements, and repair method — it does not offer an opinion on coverage or policy interpretation.
certificationText: I certify that the observations, measurements, and photographic evidence contained in this report were made by me or under my direct supervision, and accurately represent the condition of the property identified above on the date of inspection. This report constitutes a contractor scope submission and does not represent a coverage determination.
concealedConditionProcedure:
1. Stop affected work when practical.
2. Photograph and measure the condition.
3. Notify the homeowner.
4. Notify the carrier and provide a reinspection opportunity when appropriate.
5. Prepare a written change order.
6. Apply the previously disclosed unit price.
7. Obtain homeowner authorization.
8. Preserve invoices and installation photographs.

This procedure applies with particular care to concealed sheathing conditions discovered during tear-off.
forensicSummary: Based on the physical findings documented during the inspection on the date of loss, including storm-event verification, test-square hit counts, and measured damage indicators, the roof system requires full-slope replacement to restore code-compliant and reasonably uniform conditions. This conclusion is the contractor's professional assessment of the physical condition observed — it is not a coverage determination.

methodology.enforcementStatement: This inspection was captured through a software-enforced protocol. Each step below defines the evidence it requires, and the application blocked submission until every applicable requirement was satisfied. At submission the gate was re-evaluated on the server from the stored records, and every photograph was re-hashed against the submission manifest — the capturing device could not bypass either check.

inspector.name: Sam Whitfield
inspector.credentials: HAAG Certified Inspector — Residential Roofs (HAAG Engineering)

standards.testSquareProtocol: A 10′×10′ test square is chalked on each accessible directional slope. Each impact within the square is circled and photographed with a scale reference; hits are counted and classified. A documented zero-hit square is a valid finding.
standards.markingStandard: All documented damage is circled in contrasting chalk/keel and photographed wide, mid, and close (a wide/mid/close triad) with a scale reference in the close photo.
standards.photoStandard: Full-resolution originals are preserved unaltered. Every photo carries capture time (UTC), GPS coordinates, and a content hash (SHA-256) recorded at capture for chain-of-custody.

steps.name: Arrival Log
steps.description: On-site conditions (sky, wind, temp), personnel present, GPS + time.

steps.name: Property Profile
steps.description: Property & construction description — type, stories, roof age with basis, deck type.

steps.name: Elevation Walk
steps.description: A wide photo of each of the four elevations, plus the damage-found determinations.

steps.name: Roof Facets & Measurements
steps.description: Every roof facet with area, material, pitch and damage documentation, plus whole-roof linears.

steps.name: Test Squares
steps.description: A test-square photo on every facet that carries hail damage.

steps.name: Roof Components & Penetrations
steps.description: Existing components and roof penetrations, each with a photo.

steps.name: Roofing Product ID
steps.description: At least one roofing-product identification record.

steps.name: Siding Inspection
steps.description: Siding facets: damage classification, facet photo, and per-component photos.

steps.name: Collateral Sweep
steps.description: Labeled collateral photos, roof-level then ground-level.

steps.name: Interior / Attic
steps.description: Interior/attic evidence, or an explicit no-interior-claim waiver.

steps.name: Repairability Assessment
steps.description: Explicit repair-vs-replace field determination — never defaulted.

steps.name: Temporary Repairs & Mitigation
steps.description: Emergency tarping / mitigation performed, with before & after photos.

steps.name: Homeowner
steps.description: Factual homeowner intake (prior repairs, prior claims).

steps.name: Existing / Unrelated Conditions
steps.description: Pre-existing or non-storm conditions explicitly excluded from the claim.

steps.name: Declaration
steps.description: The inspector signs off on the completeness of the capture.

steps.name: Readiness & Submit
steps.description: Zero hard deficiencies remain and the package is confirmed ready.

propertySummary.accessibilityNotes: Rear elevation requires 28ft ladder; no roof hatch.

restorationReport.purposeAndScope: Storm of record confirmed: hail event on the documented date of loss Test-square counts exceed functional-damage threshold on both primary slopes Granule displacement and mat exposure documented across multiple damage instances Product identification and matching assessment per field findings Scope of work computed from field measurements at contractor incurred cost

construction.framingNotes: Rafters sound; no deflection observed at ridge.

roof.note: If not for the 5/18 hail event, this mat fracture with granule loss would not be present; the impact bruise pattern is consistent with hail, not foot traffic or manufacturing defect.
North slope impacts corroborate a multi-directional hail event consistent with the storm of record.

existingOrUnrelatedConditions.note: Pre-existing mechanical damage consistent with ladder contact; excluded from this claim.

restorationReport.recommendedScopeNarrative: Based on the physical findings documented during the inspection on the date of loss, including storm-event verification, test-square hit counts, and measured damage indicators, the roof system requires full-slope replacement to restore code-compliant and reasonably uniform conditions. This conclusion is the contractor's professional assessment of the physical condition observed — it is not a coverage determination.

weatherEvidence.causationSummary: Quarter to half-dollar size hail reported with a severe thunderstorm. (LWX)
weatherEvidence.ifNotForNote: If not for the 5/18 hail event, this mat fracture with granule loss would not be present; the impact bruise pattern is consistent with hail, not foot traffic or manufacturing defect.

repairabilityAssessment.questionPresented: Can a code-compliant, reasonably uniform repair be achieved by partial replacement?
repairabilityAssessment.methodology: Attempted controlled repair on facet F2; sourced matching material through two suppliers.
repairabilityAssessment.materialsReviewed: Existing architectural laminate; discontinued line, no ASTM-equivalent match located.
repairabilityAssessment.fieldTestFindings: A repair attempt was performed on site. Adjacent shingles fractured during the attempt, indicating brittleness. Matching material could not be sourced. The installed product is discontinued. Sealant strip failed to release cleanly at 3 of 4 attempts.
repairabilityAssessment.repairAttemptRisks: Collateral breakage to undamaged courses; compromised water-shedding at tie-in.
repairabilityAssessment.determination: Not repairable in place — full replacement required.
repairabilityAssessment.recommendation: Full replacement of affected slopes to achieve uniform, code-compliant result.
repairabilityAssessment.assessorCredentials: HAAG Certified Inspector — Residential Roofs (HAAG Engineering)

codeCitations.title: Roof replacement
codeCitations.body: Roof replacement shall include the removal of existing layers of roof coverings down to the roof deck. Required where existing coverings are damaged such that repair is not permitted.

codeCitations.title: Drip edge
codeCitations.body: A drip edge shall be provided at eaves and rake edges of shingle roofs. Adjacent segments shall be lapped and the drip edge fastened per code spacing.

codeCitations.title: Underlayment application
codeCitations.body: Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with the manufacturer’s installation instructions and the code for the roof-covering type and roof slope.

codeCitations.title: Ice barrier
codeCitations.body: In areas where there has been a history of ice forming along the eaves, an ice barrier shall be installed for asphalt shingles. Applicability is climate/jurisdiction dependent — confirm for the property’s location.

codeCitations.title: Roof sheathing / decking
codeCitations.body: Roof sheathing shall be of an approved type and thickness for the span and shall provide a sound substrate for the roof covering. Deteriorated or non-conforming decking exposed during tear-off must be replaced to receive the new covering.

temporaryRepairs.description: Emergency tarp installed over rear slope penetration.

propertyProtectionPlan.description: Solar array requires licensed de-energize/remove-reset; pool requires rigid debris shielding, not sheeting.
propertyProtectionPlan.whyOrdinaryTarpingInsufficient: Solar array requires licensed de-energize/remove-reset; pool requires rigid debris shielding, not sheeting.

measurements.description: Roof areas, squares, slopes, pitch, eaves, rakes, ridges, hips, valleys, waste factor, and roof diagram.

estimate.description: Detailed line items, quantities, pricing, taxes, overhead & profit, and area/category recaps.

priceBook.description: Standard roof-system rate, CDX/FRT rates, restricted-access rate, and other consistently applied unit pricing.

pricingSupport.description: Supplier quotes, material invoices, labor-cost build-up, and normalized comparable bids.

constructionAgreement.description: Executed agreement establishing scope, pricing method, payment terms, and change-order procedure.

permitInspection.description: Permit application, fee schedule, issued permit, inspection requests and results, and closed-permit record.

missingInputs: areasImpacted.collateral: flagged impacted but no damage or scope records were captured
photos.captureContext: some photos carry neither triadRole nor preliminaryRole
